RULING:
DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN RAYMOND CRONIN (#274286), ARVIN REECE, (#274951), NOEL SHUSTED (#275378), TED V. KENNEDY (#275392) CASES

Prior to the hearing both counsel stipulated that the same argument could be applied to all four cases: Raymond Cronin (#274286), Arvin Reece, (#274951), Noel Shusted (#275378), Ted V. Kennedy (#275392)
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for decedent's alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to decedent. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. Orders for all four cases were signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
